fields.label: Authentication method
fields.description: Choose an authentication method to access the data source

fields.label: User
fields.description: The username of the data source account

fields.label: Password
fields.description: The password of the data source account

fields.label: Add self-signed certificate
fields.description: Add your own Certificate Authority (CA) certificate on top of one generated by the certificate authorities for additional security measures

fields.label: TLS Client Authentication
fields.description: Validate using TLS client authentication, in which the server authenticates the client

fields.label: ServerName
fields.description: A Servername is used to verify the hostname on the returned certificate

fields.label: Client Certificate
fields.description: The client certificate can be generated from a Certificate Authority or be self-signed

fields.label: Client Key
fields.description: The client key can be generated from a Certificate Authority or be self-signed

ui.placeholder: Begins with --- RSA PRIVATE KEY CERTIFICATE ---

fields.label: Skip TLS certificate validation
fields.description: Skipping TLS certificate validation is not recommended unless absolutely necessary or for testing

fields.label: Allowed cookies
fields.description: Grafana proxy deletes forwarded cookies by default. Specify cookies by name that should be forwarded to the data source.

fields.label: Custom HTTP Headers
fields.description: Additional HTTP headers sent with every request. Header names are stored in jsonData (httpHeaderName<N>); header values are write-only in secureJsonData (httpHeaderValue<N>).

validations.message: Header name must start with a letter and contain only letters, digits, or hyphens.

fields.label: Minimal step
fields.description: Minimal step used for metric query. Should be the same or higher as the scrape interval setting in the Pyroscope database.

relationships.description: Basic auth credentials. Both fields are used together when root.basicAuth is true.
TLS mutual auth: enabling jsonData.tlsAuth requires serverName + client cert + client key. See @grafana/plugin-ui TLSClientAuth.tsx.
Self-signed / custom CA verification: enabling jsonData.tlsAuthWithCACert requires the CA certificate PEM in secureJsonData.tlsCACert.

instructions.msg: Pyroscope authentication is built on Grafana's HTTP settings model, not a discriminator field. The editor's three visible auth methods write these flags: 'Basic authentication' sets root.basicAuth=true and provides root.basicAuthUser + secureJsonData.basicAuthPassword; 'Forward OAuth Identity' sets jsonData.oauthPassThru=true; 'No Authentication' leaves both false. Only one of basicAuth / oauthPassThru should be true at a time — the editor's onAuthMethodSelect (@grafana/plugin-ui utils.ts:44-54) always writes both. The upstream utility convertLegacyAuthProps also recognises root.withCredentials for 'cross-site access control', but this plugin does not offer it in its visibleMethods (default [BasicAuth, OAuthForward, NoAuth]).

Minimal Basic-auth payload: {"url":"http://localhost:4040","basicAuth":true,"basicAuthUser":"<user>","jsonData":{},"secureJsonData":{"basicAuthPassword":"<password>"}}. Minimal No-auth payload: {"url":"http://localhost:4040","jsonData":{}}. Minimal OAuth-forward payload: {"url":"http://localhost:4040","jsonData":{"oauthPassThru":true}}. root.url is required — the Pyroscope backend reads settings.URL directly (pkg/grafana-pyroscope-datasource/instance.go:66) and uses it as the base URL of the Connect/gRPC-over-HTTP profiling client. Both `phlare` (aliasID at src/plugin.json:6) and `grafana-pyroscope-datasource` resolve to this plugin — provisioning with either id lands in the same code path.

TLS is independent of the auth method. jsonData.tlsAuth=true enables mTLS and requires jsonData.serverName plus secureJsonData.tlsClientCert and secureJsonData.tlsClientKey. jsonData.tlsAuthWithCACert=true enables server-cert verification against a custom CA and requires secureJsonData.tlsCACert. Both can be enabled together. jsonData.tlsSkipVerify=true bypasses verification entirely and should only be used for testing. All TLS fields are consumed by the SDK's settings.HTTPClientOptions(ctx) call in pkg/grafana-pyroscope-datasource/instance.go:52.

secureJsonData values (basicAuthPassword, tlsCACert, tlsClientCert, tlsClientKey) are write-only: they can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether a secret is already configured on an existing datasource, read the secureJsonFields object (e.g. secureJsonFields.basicAuthPassword == true) instead of secureJsonData. Custom HTTP headers ARE now modeled by the jsonData_httpHeaders array field using an indexedPair storage mapping: each header's name is written to jsonData.httpHeaderName<N> and its value is written write-only to secureJsonData.httpHeaderValue<N> (starting at N=1), following the same write-only secret pattern, exactly as @grafana/plugin-ui's CustomHeaders component and the SDK HTTPClientOptions expect. The per-header httpHeaderValue<N> secrets are dynamic and are therefore not listed among the static SecureJsonDataKeys. Pyroscope itself defines no other plugin-specific secrets beyond the shared HTTP settings set.

jsonData.minStep is the only Pyroscope-specific configuration field. It is a duration string ('15s', '1m', '500ms') matched by the regex ^\d+(ms|[Mwdhmsy])$ (ConfigEditor.tsx:65). The Pyroscope backend parses it via SDK backend/gtime.ParseDuration and uses `max(query.Interval, minStep)` as the metric-query step (pkg/grafana-pyroscope-datasource/query.go:82-89, 182-186). Empty or unparseable values fall back to 15s at query time — no error is raised. It should be the same as or higher than the Pyroscope scrape interval; too small a value produces sparse or empty series. If unset, Pyroscope's server-side default step applies.

The Pyroscope datasource plugin ships no upstream pkg/models/settings.go — its only server-side read of instance settings is settings.URL and the SDK's settings.HTTPClientOptions(ctx) call in pkg/grafana-pyroscope-datasource/instance.go:51-68 (the profiling client wraps that HTTP client). jsonData.minStep is unmarshaled ad-hoc from settings.JSONData inside query handling (query.go:74-89, 173-187). The LoadConfig utility on this entry therefore represents the intended flat shape a Grafana-side caller needs, not a mirror of an upstream loader.
